Title: Identification of a plastidial phenylalanine exporter that influences flux distribution through the phenylalanine biosynthetic network

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cationic amino-acid transporter (CAT); amino acid–polyamine–organocation (APC) transporter family; predicted 14 transmembrane helices with N-terminal plastid transit peptide
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all flower organs and leaves; lowest at bud stage, increased after flower opening; rhythmic with highest transcript abundance at night on day 2 postanthesis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all flower organs and leaves; induced during flower opening and peaks at night on day 2 postanthesis in corollas
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Clarkia breweri LIS petal-specific promoter (for RNAi and overexpression constructs)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Floral scent compounds involved in reproduction/pollinator attraction
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes plastidial export of aromatic amino acids, especially phenylalanine, thereby increasing phenylalanine-derived volatile emission and supporting phenylalanine/tyrosine accumulation and plastidial pathway flux
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression analysis; plastid-targeting GFP fusion; plastid immunoblotting; heterologous radiolabel transport assays in E. coli; petal-specific RNAi knockdown; petal-specific overexpression; metabolite profiling; stable-isotope metabolic flux analysis with 15N-tyrosine
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Daily light/dark cycle; developmental stage (bud versus day 2 postanthesis); feeding with 10 mM 15N-tyrosine for flux analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi reduced phenylalanine-derived volatile emission by 20–42% and lowered phenylalanine, tyrosine, prephenate and arogenate pools; no major change in shikimate and only minor effect on tryptophan
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of petunia corollas (6 libraries; Illumina HiSeq2500); de novo assembled contigs including Ph21511; accession number NA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate transporter for engineering flux from plastidial aromatic amino-acid biosynthesis toward floral scent or other phenylalanine-derived metabolites; useful for pathway engineering together with phenylalanine biosynthetic and utilization genes, but partial compensation by alternative cytosolic phenylpyruvate pathway should be considered
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Radiolabel assays support transport of phenylalanine, tyrosine and tryptophan, but in planta evidence and flux analysis indicate a key role in plastidial phenylalanine export influencing network-wide flux partitioning; likely affects pathway flux indirectly via organellar metabolite-mediated feedback inhibition
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ph21511/PhpCAT was identified by homology to E. coli PheP and coexpression with phenylalanine metabolic genes. 2. PhpCAT is plastid-localized and transports all three aromatic amino acids in heterologous assays. 3. RNAi suppression decreased phenylalanine-derived volatile emission and reduced phenylalanine, tyrosine, prephenate and arogenate pools. 4. Overexpression increased volatile emission and aromatic amino-acid/intermediate pools. 5. Flux analysis showed reduced plastidial arogenate-pathway flux and relatively increased cytosolic phenylpyruvate-pathway contribution when PhpCAT was downregulated.
